title: griselinia littoralis
intro: Griselinia littoralis, also known as Kapuka or New Zealand broadleaf, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. The leaves and roots of Griselinia littoralis have been used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The leaves and roots of Griselinia littoralis are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
